question.text: Under FEMA 1999, which authority is empowered to make regulations to promote orderly development and maintenance of the foreign exchange market in India?

answer.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
Foreign Exchange Dealers' Association of India (FEDAI)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FEMA 1999 (Foreign Exchange Management Act) conferred powers on the Reserve Bank of India to make regulations to carry out the provisions of the Act and to promote orderly development and maintenance of the foreign exchange market in India. **The Trap:** SEBI regulates Exchange-traded contracts, but its role is limited to exchange-traded derivative contracts — the primary and fundamental role over all foreign exchange activities belongs to RBI under FEMA. **Cross-Exam Context:** Regulatory jurisdiction — RBI vs SEBI. RBI's role is primary and fundamental covering all activities; SEBI's is limited to exchange-traded derivative contracts on foreign exchange. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the hierarchy: FEMA 1999 empowers RBI for foreign exchange market broadly; SEBI Act 1992 covers exchange-traded contracts specifically.
